researched_category.notes: Podio is a customizable work management platform by Progress (formerly Citrix) that allows teams to build no-code apps, workspaces, and automated workflows for project management, CRM, and collaboration.

one_liner.value: Customizable no-code work management platform for building apps, workspaces, and automated workflows.

methods.notes: Podio uses OAuth2 as the primary protocol with four flows: server-side, client-side, username/password, and app authentication. API keys (Client ID + Client Secret) are used to register applications, and access tokens are obtained via OAuth2 flows.

access.notes: Any developer can sign up for a free Podio account and generate API keys from the settings page. The API is free for both free and paid accounts with the same rate limits. Rate limits default to 1,000 calls/hour (250 for intensive operations). Higher rate limits require paid add-ons starting at $23/month. No admin approval or sales contact needed for basic API access.

summary.value: Comprehensive REST API (JSON) covering all Podio functionality: Applications, Items, Tasks, Files, Comments, Contacts, Spaces, Organizations, Users, Calendar, Conversations, Search, Webhooks, and more. The entire Podio frontend is built on this API. Official client libraries exist for PHP, .NET, Ruby, Java, Python, Android, and Objective-C. Also has an OpenAPI specification available.

agent_interface.notes: Podio has an official MCP server hosted at mcp.podio.com, documented at docs.sharefile.com. It provides 22 tools for reading/writing workspaces, apps, items, tasks, and notifications. The MCP server supports both shared default credentials (for testing) and custom API keys (recommended for production). Also provides official client libraries/SDKs for multiple languages.

rationale.value: Podio is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a comprehensive public REST API, an official MCP server (mcp.podio.com) with 22 tools, self-serve free API key generation, no paid plan requirement for API access, and well-documented OAuth2 authentication. The only limitations are standard rate limits (1,000 calls/hour) which can be raised with paid add-ons, but these don't block building an agent toolkit.

s1.quote: Simplify Work, Amplify Results with Progress Podio. Unify and automate work with no-code tools, flexible workflows and customizable business solutions.
s1.title: Work Management Platform | Streamline Collaboration & Workflows | Podio

s2.quote: Whether you're getting started with the Progress Podio platform or scaling it across your department, there is a plan to fit your business.
s2.title: Pricing | Flexible Plans for Teams of All Sizes | Podio

s3.quote: The Podio API uses the OAuth2 protocol for authentication. This allows external apps to request authorization to Podio without having direct access to the user's password.
s3.title: Authentication - Podio API Documentation

s4.quote: It's easy to get one or more API keys for accessing your Podio data. You just have to sign in or sign up with Podio, and go to the API key tab in your settings.
s4.title: Get API key - Podio API Documentation

s5.quote: Is the Podio API free to use? The Podio API is free to use for both premium and free Podio accounts. Both have full access to the API and have the same rate limits. There is no extra charge for using the API.
s5.title: Frequently Asked Questions - Podio API Documentation

s6.quote: You have to sign in or sign up with Podio. Then you find your API keys as a tab under your account settings.
s6.title: Get API key - Podio API Documentation

s7.quote: How do I get an API key? You have to sign in or sign up with Podio. Then you find your API keys as a tab under your account settings.
s7.title: Frequently Asked Questions - Podio API Documentation

s8.quote: API Integrations: 1000 calls per day [for Free, Plus, and Premium plans]
s8.title: Pricing | Flexible Plans for Teams of All Sizes | Podio

s9.quote: Scale usage with more API calls per hour to support higher integration needs. Add-ons are available on paid plans only and billed with your Podio subscription.
s9.title: Pricing | Flexible Plans for Teams of All Sizes | Podio

s10.quote: Our API is split up into different areas, that each contains a subset of methods grouped by functionality. ... Applications, Comments, Contacts, Files, Hooks, Items, OAuth authorization, Status, Tasks, Actions, Alerts, App market, Batch, Calendar, Conversations, Devices, Email, Embeds, Flows, Forms, Friends, Grants, Importer, Integrations, Layout, Linked Accounts, Notifications, Organizations, Questions, Ratings, Recurrence, Reference, Reminders, Search, Space members, Spaces, Stream, Subscriptions, Tags, Tasks, Users, Views, Voting, Widgets
s10.title: Podio API Documentation

s11.quote: The API is available at https://api.podio.com. It is RESTful and uses json as the exchange format. SSL is mandatory and used for all communication. OAuth2 is used for authorization and authentication.
s11.title: Concepts and conventions: Conventions and structure of the API

s12.quote: The Podio API is a complete programmable interface to all Podio functionality. We currently provide wrappers for PHP, .NET, Ruby, Java, Python, Android and Objective-C which make working with common API operations very simple.
s12.title: Developers - Podio API Documentation

s13.quote: REST API providing complete programmatic access to Podio apps, items, workspaces, organizations, users, tasks, files, comments, and webhooks. Authentication uses OAuth2
s13.title: Podio REST API — Documentation, OpenAPI

s14.quote: The Podio MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server connects AI assistants — ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot Studio, Claude Desktop, and any other MCP-compatible platform — directly to your Podio data.
s14.title: Podio MCP Server — Integration Guide | Podio

s15.quote: The Podio MCP Server gives your AI assistant 22 tools covering both reading and writing your Podio data: Browse, Read data, Write data, Tasks, Notifications
s15.title: Podio MCP Server — Integration Guide | Podio

s16.quote: The Podio API only allows you to make a certain amount of API calls each hour. The general limit is 1,000 API calls per hour, but if the API call is marked as 'Rate limited' in the API reference the call is deemed resource intensive and a lower rate of 250 calls per hour is enforced.
s16.title: Concepts and conventions: Rate limits

limitations: Rate limits page (developers.podio.com/rate-limits) returned HTTP 500; used alternative URL (developers.podio.com/index/limits) for rate limit info.
No direct Podio toolkit found in Composio catalogue; only a third-party Podio automation tool (ProcFu) is listed.
The OpenAPI spec URL from apis.io was not verified directly.
